chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 826 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصَلِّي وَ لاَ يُخْرِجُ يَدَيْهِ مِنْ ثَوْبِهِ فَقَالَ «إِنْ أَخْرَجَ يَدَيْهِ فَهُوَ حَسَنٌ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يُخْرِجْ يَدَيْهِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.826 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) about a man praying without taking his hands out of his garment.
The Imam said: "If he takes his hands out, it is good, but if he does not, there is no issue (no problem)."
